source_jobs.title: AI Operations Associate, California
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEVOYA:


Nevoya is North America's leading next-generation electric trucking carrier, transforming logistics through AI-native operations and relentless customer focus. The company builds and uses proprietary technology to deliver zero-emissions carrier services to Fortune 500 shippers with superior economics and service to legacy diesel operators. The company has operations in California and Texas, and is backed by globally-renowned investors including Lowercarbon Capital.





ABOUT THE ROLE:

This is a foundational hire. You will do different aspects of dispatching in trucking — without needing any trucking experience. You’ll keep real freight moving for real customers: assigning loads, communicating with drivers, handling exceptions, and solving the problems of the day.



The reason this works is you. You are AI-native — AI is your default way of researching, troubleshooting, and getting work done — and you have the critical thinking to notice when the playbook is wrong and the initiative to propose the fix. You’ll work directly with engineering and product to automate the repetitive parts of the job, so the role keeps getting smarter.



You’ll get a lot of direction, coaching, and ownership early. Do this well and the role is a launchpad: people who build the operational foundation here will grow into bigger roles across the company.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Run daily dispatch execution from our playbook: load assignment, driver communication, check-calls, and exception handling

 - Keep loads covered and customers informed, escalating with judgment when something can’t wait

 - Use AI tools daily to research, troubleshoot, draft, analyze, and automate your own work

 - Improve the playbook as you go — document what works, flag what doesn’t, and ship a better process every week

 - Call out problems early and come with proposed solutions, not just escalations

 - Partner with engineering and product to turn recurring manual work into automated workflows

 - Track the KPIs of your operation — load coverage, on-time performance, driver utilization — and use them to improve


WHAT YOU’LL BRING:

 - A recent degree or 1–2 years of work experience — high-performing new grads encouraged to apply

 - AI-native habits: you already use AI as your default tool for learning, research, and producing work

 - Sharp critical thinking — you spot what’s broken, ask why, and bring a proposed answer

 - Hungry and coachable: you want direction, feedback, and a steep learning curve

 - Strong written and verbal communication — you’ll be talking to drivers, customers, and engineers in the same hour

 - Organized and calm under pressure; comfortable with ambiguity and shifting priorities

 - No trucking, logistics, or dispatch experience required


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Internship or early experience in operations, logistics, supply chain, or a fast-moving startup

 - Comfort with data — spreadsheets, SQL, or light scripting

 - Spanish language proficiency, a meaningful asset in our operating markets




AT NEVOYA, WE BELIEVE DIVERSE TEAMS BUILD BETTER SOLUTIONS.


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating an inclusive and welcoming environment for all employees. We consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.



If you're excited about our mission but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be the right candidate for this or another role.



If you have any other questions please email our Recruiting Team directly @ careers@nevoya.com http://nevoya.com

source_jobs.title: VP of Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEVOYA:


Nevoya is North America's leading next-generation electric trucking carrier, transforming logistics through AI-native operations and relentless customer focus. The company builds and uses proprietary technology to deliver zero-emissions carrier services to Fortune 500 shippers with superior economics and service to legacy diesel operators. The company has operations in California and Texas, and is backed by globally-renowned investors including Lowercarbon Capital.

About The Role:

Nevoya is building the infrastructure that will power the next generation of commercial freight, and we're growing fast. As we scale, we need an operator who can bring structure, clarity, and execution to the functions that keep the business running. That's where you come in.

As VP of Operations, you'll report directly to the CEO and own the internal business functions that enable Nevoya to move quickly without breaking things. That includes HR, finance, payroll, legal, compliance, and executive support. This is a high-ownership, hands on role that sits at the intersection of strategy and execution, and you'll be the person leadership turns to when something needs to get built, fixed, or scaled.

This is not a purely strategic seat. You'll inherit some existing team members and processes, and you'll also be expected to identify gaps and build out what's missing. The ideal candidate has a track record of operating across multiple functions at once, a bias toward action, and the organizational instincts to keep a fast-moving company on solid footing.

What You'll Do:

 - Own and oversee day-to-day operations across HR, finance, payroll, legal and executive support

 - Serve as a key thought partner to the CEO on organizational health, resource allocation, and internal operations strategy

 - Manage and develop a cross-functional team spanning multiple disciplines, with additional headcount to be added as the business scales

 - Oversee payroll operations ensuring accuracy, compliance, and a smooth employee experience

 - Support financial operations including budget tracking, vendor management, and internal reporting

 - Dig into the financials directly, from budgets and forecasts to the line-item detail, rather than relying solely on others to interpret the numbers

 - Own financial reporting and analysis, using the numbers to surface issues and guide decisions

 - Roll up your sleeves on day-to-day finance operations, staying close enough to the detail to catch what others might miss

 - Lead people operations, including onboarding, benefits administration, HR policy, and regulatory compliance

 - Manage relationships with external legal counsel, coordinating on contracts, employment matters, regulatory questions, and any litigation or risk-related issues

 - Collaborate with outside legal partners to ensure the business stays ahead of compliance requirements, particularly in the transportation and freight space

 - Manage and support executive assistants, ensuring leadership bandwidth is protected and day-to-day logistics run smoothly

 - Build and refine internal systems and processes that can scale alongside the company

 - Identify operational gaps and implement solutions before they become problems

 - Serve as a cross-functional escalation point for operational issues across departments

 - Partner with department heads to ensure their operational needs are met and resources are appropriately allocated

What You'll Bring To The Table:

 - Proven experience leading or overseeing multiple business functions simultaneously, including at least two of the following: HR, Finance or Operations

 - Strong people leadership skills with experience managing and developing cross-functional teams

 - Demonstrated ability to operate at both a strategic and tactical level without losing momentum in either direction

 - High comfort level in a startup or high-growth environment where priorities shift and ambiguity is the norm

 - Strong unde

source_jobs.title: AI Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEVOYA:


Nevoya is North America's leading next-generation electric trucking carrier, transforming logistics through AI-native operations and relentless customer focus. The company builds and uses proprietary technology to deliver zero-emissions carrier services to Fortune 500 shippers with superior economics and service to legacy diesel operators. The company has operations in California and Texas, and is backed by globally-renowned investors including Lowercarbon Capital.

About The Role:

As an AI Product Manager, you will own the discovery, design, and delivery of AI-powered products and workflows that make freight smarter. You will identify where AI can solve real operational problems, partner closely with engineering and operations to build solutions that hold up in production, and turn complex logistics challenges into products that move freight more efficiently. This is a hands-on, cross-functional role for someone who thinks in systems, leads with curiosity, and ships with speed.

Reporting to the VP of Product and AI, you will work alongside engineering and operations teams to bring AI capabilities from idea to launch. Beyond shipping product, you will help stand up Nevoya's product operations function and contribute to how the company approaches AI enablement as the platform continues to evolve. You will have real ownership early and the room to shape how AI products get built here.

This is a role built for a builder who is equally comfortable in a product strategy conversation and a sprint planning session. You bring a strong point of view on how great AI products get made and a genuine interest in how emerging capabilities can be applied to the realities of freight. Nevoya aims to reinvent the trucking industry and this role will be a key player to achieve our vision.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Own AI-focused product initiatives from discovery through launch, working hand in hand with the engineering team

 - Identify and prioritize where AI can add meaningful value across freight workflows, building a clear roadmap of opportunities

 - Conduct and synthesize user research to surface high-value problems and validate where AI is the right solution

 - Define product requirements and success metrics for AI features, holding the team accountable to outcomes over outputs

 - Partner with engineering to translate complex logistics challenges into reliable, production-ready AI products

 - Contribute to the development of Nevoya's broader AI strategy as that function grows

 - Support the buildout of the product operations function, including release processes, feedback loops, and cross-functional rituals

 - Own voice-of-customer for the operators who live in the product, spending time close to real freight workflows before specing the tools and AI features that support them

 - Develop tooling and workflows that improve how AI products are evaluated, shipped, and monitored in production

 - Work with internal teams to ensure smooth launches and strong adoption

 - Communicate product direction and tradeoffs clearly across technical and non-technical stakeholders


WHAT YOU'LL BRING TO THE TABLE:

 - 3 - 6 years of product experience with a focus on AI operations, with a track record of shipping in fast-moving environments

 - Direct experience building, shipping, or operationalizing AI-powered products or workflows

 - Strong instincts for user research and the ability to translate customer insights into clear product direction

 - Demonstrated ability to partner closely with engineering teams and lead product development end to end

 - Ability to go down to the operating level, figure problems, build solutions that meet business goals working with operations, dispatch, drivers, and leadership (a not taks is too small for me mentality)

 - Comfort with ambiguity and a track record of bringing structure to early-stage or scaling product environments

 - Strong systems thinking, including comfort designing around a

notes: AI-native operations and logistics company with a strong focus on product development
